task_completion.description: A .dockerignore file is created to exclude unnecessary files from the build context.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., 'cat .dockerignore') showing a .dockerignore file in /home/ga/build-project that explicitly lists '.git' and '__pycache__'.

task_completion.description: System package installations are reordered to prevent cache invalidation from source code changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the Dockerfile where the 'RUN apt-get ...' instruction is placed before the main source code copy instruction (e.g., 'COPY . .').

task_completion.description: The requirements.txt file is copied separately before installing Python dependencies.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the Dockerfile where 'COPY requirements.txt ...' appears before the 'RUN pip install ...' instruction.

task_completion.description: Python package installations are reordered to prevent cache invalidation from source code changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the Dockerfile where the 'RUN pip install ...' instruction is placed before the main source code copy instruction (e.g., 'COPY . .').

task_completion.description: The optimized Docker image is successfully built with the correct tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing the execution of 'docker build -t build-project:optimized .' and its successful completion output.

task_completion.description: The built image is run as a container and is accessible on port 5000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing 'docker run' with port mapping (e.g., '-p 5000:5000'), 'docker ps' listing the container on port 5000, or a successful 'curl' command to localhost:5000.

integrity.description: Files were genuinely modified using an editor or standard terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) or valid shell commands (echo, sed) being used to edit the Dockerfile and .dockerignore, rather than just printing the final expected text.

integrity.description: The Docker build process was actually executed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows genuine Docker build output with layers being cached or executed, not just a typed out success message.

integrity.description: The container was genuinely tested or verified.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows actual system output from 'docker run', 'docker ps', or 'curl', rather than manually typed mock responses.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/build-project. The target image tag must be exactly 'build-project:optimized'. The container must expose and run on port 5000. The Dockerfile must be optimized by placing 'apt-get' and 'pip install' commands before the main source code copy ('COPY . .'). 'requirements.txt' must be copied before 'pip install'. A '.dockerignore' file must exist and contain '.git' and '__pycache__'.
